session_1.text: Fourteen thousand years old. I bet that's a lot of women.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Well, I'm taking Edith home. Sandy?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: ...Are you sorry for some of those things you said?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Well. Like a good Christian, I...

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (hugs John, he lifts her up giving her a tight hug)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Oh! Well, you did a terrible thing, but we're all so thankful you're all right. Even Art- He just hates things he can't understand.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: You're a sadist, John, but I admit I got a kick out of chasing my tail around your maypole...

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: even if that is all I caught. Good luck to you.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Edith puts her hand on John's face, and gives him a kiss on the cheek. They depart

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Dan comes out, hugs Sandy from the side.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: I don't know, man. Something about this...

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Something about you, John. The more I think about it, the more I'm no longer in that Chinese box.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: I sense...space. A kinda latitude of what we happily call reality, in which, as everybody keeps saying... anything's possible.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: No. N-no. N-no. No- No more words. I'm gonna go home, and I'm gonna watch Doctor Who for a dose of sanity. Good luck to you, man, wherever this may lead you. ...You drop me a line, sometime.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Let me know how you're making out.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Dan gives Sandy a friendly kiss and walks off.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: So, John Oldman. What other pun names have you used?

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Will is slowly exiting, inadvertently sneaking up on them.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Lots. John Paley for John Paleolithic. John Savage- Got really crazy about 60 years ago, when I was teaching at Harvard, I was John Thomas Partee.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: John T. Partee- Boston tea party- Will who is right behind them now takes a step back with a look of shock on his face

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Wait, wait, wait. B-Boston? 60 years ago? J-John Partee?

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: John puts a hand on his mouth.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: You did not teach chemistry! I do not believe you!

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: My- My- My dog's name. We had him before I was born.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Sorry, I had to move on. You know that. I left enough. I left enough.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (putting his arm around him and escorting him inside)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Chilly Willy, always cold. Never could stand the cold.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Wait, you- you had a beard!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Yeah, you used to tug on it to see if it was real.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Will! God. Call an ambulance, now!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (Will gasps, collapses with John holding him)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Come on, Willy. Will. Come on, buddy.
